spec_match.rationale: The demo clearly shows how text-fit: balance scales captions of various lengths to match the card width.

interactive.rationale: Interactivity relies on browser window resizing. The on-page 'live probe' interaction is disconnected from the actual text-fit demonstration.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb accurately describes the concept of using text-fit: balance to align visual weight of text and image.

edge_cases.rationale: It effectively covers multiple text lengths (1 to 5+ words) and provides a clear side-by-side comparison with the non-standard fallback.

references.rationale: The page includes the required ChromeStatus reference link in the see also section.

design_system.rationale: The demo uses the design system's CSS variables and markup conventions perfectly.

openQuestions.title: Add resizable container slider to card grid
openQuestions.detail: The caption fitter demo currently relies on browser window resizing to show the dynamic scaling of captions. Adding a width slider (similar to the Headline Scaler demo) would allow users to easily inspect how the balance behavior adapts to different card widths directly within the page.

openQuestions.title: Refine capability probe for text-fit
openQuestions.detail: The 'Run capability probe' button checks general APIs like customElements and navigator.gpu, but does not check for text-fit support. It should use `CSS.supports('text-fit', 'balance')` to report whether the browser actually supports the feature being demonstrated.

summary: A solid demo that visually shows the benefit of text-fit: balance for card layouts. However, it lacks direct on-page interactivity for the scaling itself, and the capability probe script is generic and does not target the feature.
